title: COMPAT — NAICS 221118 Other Electric Power Generation (BESS Reference)

sections.title: 1 — Summary
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Type | Threshold | Verdict | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | operational | — | **none** | Site selection is the market's core activity — IPPs self-select connection-capable sites |
| C2 | Land Footprint & Siting | physical | 40–80 m²/MWh | **none** | IPPs acquire greenfield/brownfield plots sized exactly for BESS footprint |
| C3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | regulatory | VdS / Feuerwehr | **none** | Standard BESS IPP site design already incorporates VdS concept + Baugenehmigung |
| C4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | physical | — | **none** | Containerized BESS with NOVEC + deflagration venting is the market's default architecture |
| C5 | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | regulatory | 6–12 months | **none** | Parallelized with procurement; baseline expectation for 221118 project schedule |
| C6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | economic | — | **mitigable** | CRMA/EU Battery Reg + FX/tariff exposure adds compliance overhead; dual-source procurement + EU-local-content strategy |
| C7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | environmental | -20 to +50 °C | **none** | German site ambient (-15 to +38 °C) sits well inside envelope; HVAC handles peaks |
| C8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | physical | 10–15% | **none** | Baked into IPP revenue model — arbitrage/FCR/aFRR models price 85–90% RTE as baseline |
| C9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | economic | €250–400/kWh | **none** | 221118 IPP market specifically exists inside this envelope (LFP container pricing 2026 ≈ €200–320/kWh installed) |

---

sections.title: 2 — Knockouts
sections.content:
**None.** This is the product's native market (distance 1 in architecture scoring). No physics or regulation knocks out the fit.

---

sections.title: 3 — Mitigable constraints (carried into scoring)
sections.content:
### C6 — Battery Supply Chain Concentration (economic)

- **Barrier:** LFP cells concentrated in CN suppliers (CATL, EVE, BYD, Gotion). FX/tariff exposure and EU Battery Regulation / CRMA compliance overhead. IPP asset owners increasingly require supply-chain disclosure.
- **Mitigation actions:**
  1. Dual-source procurement framework (primary CN + secondary KR/EU via Samsung SDI, LG ES, Northvolt/ACC as they scale)
  2. EU Battery Regulation Digital Product Passport (DPP) compliance pipeline at FAT
  3. CRMA-compatible sourcing documentation package bundled into EPC deliverables
- **Cost estimate:** **medium** (€10K–100K per project — passport tooling, audit overhead, second-source qualification)
- **Time estimate:** 3–6 months one-time organizational build; marginal per-project thereafter
- **Rationale for keeping "mitigable" not "none":** IPP buyers (esp. EU-finance-backed) are beginning to require supply-chain transparency as procurement criterion (post-CRMA 2025). Not a physical barrier, but a contractual/compliance overhead that differentiates integrators.

---

sections.title: 4 — Verdict rationale details (per-constraint)
sections.content:
### C1 — MV Grid Connection Capacity — none
IPP developers underwrite projects only after a positive Netzverträglichkeitsprüfung. The market's operating model is to pre-qualify sites with available MV feeder capacity. Mein Solar's Grid-Capacity Study L5 adjacency directly addresses this in its upstream scope.

### C2 — Land Footprint & Siting — none
221118 projects are intentionally sited on industrial plots (40–80 m²/MWh × 9 MWh ≈ 360–720 m²). Site acquisition is a distinct L6a activity and is not a product constraint at market-entry level.

### C3 — Fire-Protection Clearance — none
VdS fire concept + Feuerwehr access are baseline deliverables in every BESS IPP project in Germany. Mein Solar has the competence (PARTIAL → bringing VdS-certified engineer in-house per 04e). Not a barrier to market participation — it **is** the market's standard practice.

### C4 — Li-Ion Thermal Runaway — none
Containerized BESS = NOVEC 1230 + deflagration venting + compartmentalization is the **de facto** standard the IPP market operates under. The constraint is industry-baseline, not market-specific.

### C5 — Grid Code Certification Lead Time — none
Every 221118 project schedule includes 6–12 months of FGW TR8 dossier preparation on the critical path. This is market-routine. Mein Solar has native VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 capability (PV-EPC heritage).

### C7 — Ambient Temperature Envelope — none
German site climate: nominal -15 to +38 °C (historic extremes), squarely inside the -20 to +50 °C envelope. Internal HVAC handles any overshoot. Irrelevant for 221118 in DE geography.

### C8 — Round-Trip Energy Losses — none
IPP revenue models (day-ahead arbitrage, FCR, aFRR) explicitly price in 85–90% AC-AC RTE. Not a barrier; it's a design parameter.

### C9 — CAPEX Viability Threshold — none
The 221118 IPP market **defines itself** by projects that close the business case within €250–400/kWh. 2026 market LFP turnkey pricing ≈ €200–320/kWh installed — well inside envelope. Merchant trading stack (Entrix, Next Kraftwerke) is actively compressing payback.

---

sections.title: 5 — Downstream implications
sections.content:
| Downstream Step | Impact |
|---|---|
| Market survives Step 08 | ✅ YES |
| Goes into Step 09 Feature-Market Fit | ✅ |
| Goes into Step 10 Competitive Landscape | ✅ (vs. Fluence, Tesla, Sungrow, Wärtsilä) |
| Constraint cost penalty into Step 12 | **Low** — only 1 mitigable (C6 medium, one-time organizational) |
| Fit Assessment CR dimension | High constraint readiness — all hard physical/regulatory constraints already handled as part of baseline delivery |

---

sections.title: 6 — Graph writes (Neo4j pattern)
sections.content:
```cypher
// C6 only (knockouts & none are not stored as incompatible_with)
MERGE (c:Constraint {name: "Battery Supply Chain Concentration", run_id: $run_id})
MERGE (m:Market {naics_code: "221118", run_id: $run_id})
MERGE (c)-[r:incompatible_with]->(m)
SET r.compatibility = "mitigable",
    r.rationale = "EU Battery Reg + CRMA + FX exposure on CN-concentrated LFP supply",
    r.mitigation_actions = $actions,
    r.mitigation_cost_estimate = "medium",
    r.mitigation_time_estimate = "3-6 months",
    r.knockout_reason = null

// No Market.status change — 221118 remains active
```

---
